title: NATO Allies 'Jointly Discussing' Hormuz Closure Response, Rutte Says
detail: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte stated that allies are 'intensely discussing' how to contend with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz as energy costs continued to climb. Rutte said he expected NATO members to come together to help secure the strait, noting that European countries were not given advance notice of the US-Israel war against Iran and had needed time to respond. Stars and Stripes reported Rutte acknowledged no formal NATO consensus had been reached, while he remained confident allies would ultimately align with Washington's position.

title: Seven US Allies Back Potential Strait of Hormuz Political Coalition
detail: Seven US allies — including the UK — announced in a joint statement their support for a potential coalition to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for commercial ships and oil tankers. Japan joined at the last minute. However, the statement included no commitment to send naval vessels or other resources and was described by analysts as largely a gesture to placate President Trump. UK diplomacy had been driving the effort to assemble the broadest possible statement of political support short of military commitment.
